items.title: Gurriers at The Button Factory, Dublin
items.summary: The post Gurriers at The Button Factory, Dublin appeared first on Goldenplec.com Gurriers at The Button Factory, Dublin on Friday December 19th, 2026 Gurriers, the last diamond to be forged from Ireland’s post-punk scene before the mine collapsed, played two sold-out shows in The Button Factory this week on a landmark occasion for a group who have outgrown their sweaty club origins.

items.title: Kingfishr at 3Arena, Dublin
items.summary: The post Kingfishr at 3Arena, Dublin appeared first on Goldenplec.com   Kingfishr at 3Arena, Dublin on December 18th 2025 One of the most anticipated concerts of the year occurred on an absolutely freezing Thursday night, with Kingfishr taking the stage of the 3arena for the first of their two sold out shows.

items.summary: The Ocean's new lineup shows their worth with the dynamic "Belligerence," covering ground from hardcore and thrash to prog and doom over its 10-minute span. Heavy Song of the Week: The Ocean Deliver a Dynamic Banger with “Belligerence” Langdon Hickman

items.title: Murder accused 'acted out of instinct' when he fatally stabbed man who 'trespassed' on his Dublin home, court hears
items.summary: Derek Boyd is on trial at the Central Criminal Court charged with the murder of Mark Carroll, while his partner Chantelle Harcourt stands accused of impeding his prosecution

items.title: Dublin's Coombe Hospital celebrates 200th anniversary
items.summary: Held at the hospital on Cork Street in Dublin 8, the event featured the unveiling of a series of commemorative flags created through a collaboration between hospital staff and students from the National College of Art and Design (NCAD)

items.title: Pavement Return To The Road With A Very Unserious “Stairway” Cover
items.summary: Oakland, CA's Mosswood Meltdown has expanded to a three-day event for 2026, and last night it kicked off with a Pre-Party featuring Christina's Trip, Memo PST, Vivian Girls, Wednesday, and headliners Pavement. It was the '90s indie icons' first show since November, so we're now in year six of their second reunion era. The post Pavement Return To The Road With A Very Unserious “Stairway” Cover appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Crash Of Rhinos Play First Show In 13 Years
items.summary: On Friday (July 17), Crash Of Rhinos played their first show since 2013. In Nottingham, the band kicked of a short tour in support of Logbook, the Derby emo revivalists' first album since that year. The post Crash Of Rhinos Play First Show In 13 Years appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Martin Garrix Brings Out The Edge To Debut Latest U2 Collab “Fireflies” At Tomorrowland
items.summary: Last week, U2 released their new song "Street Of Dreams," which was first teased in May with a music video shoot in the streets of Mexico City. On Friday (July 17), the Edge joined Dutch DJ Martin Garrix onstage at Antwerp's Tomorrowland festival to debut a new collab called "Fireflies." The post Martin Garrix Brings Out The Edge To Debut Latest U2 Collab “Fireflies” At Tomorrowland appeared first on Stereogum.
